text: Code reviews are an essential part of software development. During a code review, a developer checks a colleague's work before it is added to the main project. This process helps catch bugs early, when they are simpler and cheaper to fix. Instead of finding problems after release, the team can solve them immediately. Code reviews also support knowledge sharing, as team members learn different approaches and improve their skills. By discussing each other's code, the whole team raises its quality. Regular code reviews build trust and create a culture of collaboration. In short, this practice leads to stronger, more reliable software.
